title: Single Nuclei RNA Sequencing of Tendon Tissue - small tissue biopsies
author: Adam Cribbs
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.9sth6en
materials: Pre-cool all solutions and equipment (including centrifuge (+4 o C), homogeniser (+4 o C), dissection Tray (-20 o C), scissors (-20 o C) and scalpel/razor blade (-20 o C)`). All steps in the protocol should be performed on ice or in a cold room (4 o C) to minimise RNA degradation
Proceed to FACS sorting of nuclei.

steps.title: Chop up tissue into 4 pieces...
steps.instruction: Chop up tissue into 4 pieces on a cold dissection tray. Use scissors and razor blades that have been chilled to -20oC. The best approach to begin with is to use a razor blade and shave off a small piece of tissue. Then cut this tissue into smaller pieces and set aside on the dissection tray. Sometimes tendon can be very “stringy” and this may require the use of dissection scissors.

steps.title: Pipette the tissue up and down...
steps.instruction: Pipette the tissue up and down 10 times using a wide bore P1000 tip (Slightly cut the top of the tip off a P1000).

steps.title: Transfer the sample to a 7mL or smaller Dounce homogeniser
steps.instruction: Transfer the sample to a 7mL or smaller Dounce homogeniser. (If any sample remains in the dissection tray add NL1 buffer to this and add to dounce)

steps.title: Homogenise the sample using pestle A and stroke 20 times
steps.instruction: Homogenise the sample using pestle A and stroke 20 times.

steps.title: Place the homogeniser back onto ice and wait 5 mins
steps.instruction: Place the homogeniser back onto ice and wait 5 mins. 00:05:00

steps.title: Optional
steps.instruction: Optional: Homogenise the sample using pestle B and stroke 20 times.

steps.title: Filter the sample into a 50mL...
steps.instruction: Filter the sample into a 50mL Falcon Tube passing through a 200uM strainer.

steps.title: Incubate on ice for 5 mins
steps.instruction: Incubate on ice for 5 mins. 00:05:00

steps.title: Centrifuge the tube at 500g for 5mins at 4oC
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the tube at 500g for 5mins at 4oC. 500 x g, 4 °C, 5 minutes

steps.title: Remove the supernatant and resuspend in 4mL of NL2 buffer
steps.instruction: Remove the supernatant and resuspend in 4mL of NL2 buffer.

steps.title: Incubate on ice for 5 mins
steps.instruction: Incubate on ice for 5 mins. 00:05:00

steps.title: Filter the sample into a 50mL...
steps.instruction: Filter the sample into a 50mL Falcon Tube passing through a 40uM strainer.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 500g for 5 mins at 4oC
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 500g for 5 mins at 4oC. 500 x g, 4 °C, 5 minutes

steps.title: Resuspend pellet in 2mL of NW buffer
steps.instruction: Resuspend pellet in 2mL of NW buffer.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 500g for 5 mins at 4oC
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 500g for 5 mins at 4oC. 500 x g, 4 °C, 5 minutes

steps.title: Resuspend pellet in 0
steps.instruction: Resuspend pellet in 0.5-1mL of Cell sorting buffer.

steps.title: FACS purification was performed using a Sony SH800 cell sorter
steps.instruction: FACS purification was performed using a Sony SH800 cell sorter. With our current laser settings we are unable to sort DAPI so have relied on DAPI staining of nuclei and sorting based on FSC and FL3. An example gate is shown below of a sample that shows a high level of debri: Sample showing high levels of debris SH800S cell sorter (Sony, SH800S, https://www.sonybiotechnology.com/us/instruments/sh800s-cell-sorter/specifications/, https://s3.amazonaws.com/protocols-files/public/7923b1ebcdb7e54afe7c9c3e9df9d06198d9758434a79283724ccb459e7b931a/bure5vew.png )

steps.title: Sort nuclei into FACS tubes containing 1mL of NW buffer
steps.instruction: Sort nuclei into FACS tubes containing 1mL of NW buffer.

steps.title: Centrifuge nuclei at 300g for 10 mins
steps.instruction: Centrifuge nuclei at 300g for 10 mins.

steps.title: Resuspend in appropriate volume of NW...
steps.instruction: Resuspend in appropriate volume of NW buffer so that there is a ~1000 nuclei/uL. A reliable nuclei count can be difficult, but we suggest that this is performed using trypan blue and confirmed using an automatic cell counter if available.

steps.title: Nuclei that have been counted are...
steps.instruction: Nuclei that have been counted are now ready for loading into a 10X or drop-seq experiment, according to the manufacturers conditions.

steps.title: Add 2-4 ml of NL1 buffer...
steps.instruction: Add 2-4 ml of NL1 buffer (We usually start with 2ml and then add more if required)

steps.title: Scrape the tissue into a dounce...
steps.instruction: Scrape the tissue into a dounce homogeniser from the dissection tray.
